explanation:
The natural language states that once 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, every step of the self-test (FUN.6) can be skipped or rerun individually. Since the available atomic propositions relevant to skipping self-test procedures are testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testFl2Skip, testPSExpSkip, and testAlarmsSkip, we interpret 'every step ... can be skipped' as each of these skip actions being allowed after resumeVentilation. In LTL, 'If resumeVentilation is true, then from that point on, it's always possible to skip any test step' is captured as: after resumeVentilation occurs, from that point forward, any (or all) of the skip actions can occur (i.e., are always allowed / enabled). Since LTL expresses system behavior rather than permissions, it is standard to translate this as: 'If resumeVentilation is selected, then eventually (at least once, but possibly more) any skip of any self-test is allowed.'

Thus, for each skip action, once resumeVentilation is selected, it is eventually possible to skip it (interpretation: it becomes enabled), and for all skips this should hold. This is captured by: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testFl2Skip & F testPSExpSkip & F testAlarmsSkip)). This ensures that after selecting resumeVentilation, each self-test skip can happen at least once (aligning with the 'can be skipped individually' part), and they can be skipped in any order or even rerun (not forbidden or mandatory).
output_LTL: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testFl2Skip & F testPSExpSkip & F testAlarmsSkip))

output_LTL: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testFl2Skip & F testPSExpSkip & F testAlarmsSkip))

explanation: The input statement says that if 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually. Given the atomic propositions, we can assume that tests such as 'testOxygenSensorSkip', 'testLeaksSkip', 'testAlarmsSkip', 'testPowerSwitchSkip', 'testPSExpSkip', and 'testFl2Skip' represent skip actions for individual self-test steps. Thus, whenever 'resumeVentilation' holds (i.e., is selected), it is always possible (i.e., permitted) for any (or all) of these skip actions to be enabled, and there is no requirement to force replay or enforce completion of a particular self-test step. Because LTL is not a permission logic, we can capture that 'if resumeVentilation is true, then always (globally) the skip actions for all these tests may occur at any time'. To express this, we use an implication: 'If resumeVentilation holds, then it is always possible that each test can be skipped (i.e., eventually, at any point in the future, a skip for each is possible)'. There is no atomic proposition for 'rerun', so we focus on the skipping component only. Thus, the property can be defined as: 'G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testAlarmsSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testPSExpSkip & F testFl2Skip))'. This means that whenever 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, eventually each self-test step can be skipped. Note: LTL cannot explicitly express 'optionally rerun individually' without a corresponding atomic proposition, so our formula best captures what the available propositions allow. Previous LTL statements that used implications without the eventually operator (F) or failed to include all individual skips would be too restrictive or incomplete.
output_LTL: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testAlarmsSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testPSExpSkip & F testFl2Skip))

explanation:
The natural language states: 'If “Resume Ventilation” is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually.' In LTL, we interpret this as: whenever 'resumeVentilation' occurs, it enables the skipping of the self-test steps (each of which is represented by 'testOxygenSensorSkip', 'testLeaksSkip', 'testPowerSwitchSkip', 'testPSExpSkip', 'testFl2Skip', 'testAlarmsSkip'). Since the steps can be skipped individually and there's no prohibition or exclusivity (i.e., skipping is permitted but not forced for all or none), we capture this as: globally, whenever 'resumeVentilation' holds, it is possible at any point afterwards that any or all of the skip actions are performed. In LTL, we can represent this permissiveness as: globally, if 'resumeVentilation' occurs, then eventually each of the step-skipping propositions could be become true (eventually, optionally).

This is captured by: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testPSExpSkip & F testFl2Skip & F testAlarmsSkip))

This property means: 'If resume ventilation is selected, then, at some point in the future (possibly immediately), each of the skip actions is permitted.' Since there is no LTL way to directly require 'optionality' (which is a lack of requirement!), this 'eventually' is the best translation, representing that each can be triggered or not, but it is allowed after resumeVentilation is selected.
output_LTL: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testPSExpSkip & F testFl2Skip & F testAlarmsSkip))
